verdict.summary: ENTERO scores 38/100 and, more importantly, fails the Trend Template gate (200 DMA not confirmed rising ≥1 month, RS percentile only 4). Per Prime Directive 4, gate failure terminates candidacy regardless of any fundamental positives — this is a hard AVOID, not a 'weak buy'. Revenue trends are decent but earnings quality is compromised by other-income contribution and negative operating cash conversion, and institutional sponsorship is actively declining.
strengths: Sales growth strong and accelerating (25-43% YoY range, 3-yr CAGR ~26%)
Debt levels moderate (D/E 0.40) and dilution minimal (share count +2.3% over 2yrs)
Promoter holding stable at 52.42% across 4 quarters
weaknesses: Trend Template fails on c3 (200 DMA not confirmed rising) and c8 (RS percentile 4, deeply weak) — stock is a laggard, not a leader
Earnings quality poor: OCF/PAT 3yr ratio negative (-0.13), indicating profits not converting to cash
Other income contributed 17-48% of PBT in 6 of last 8 quarters — reported EPS growth partly non-operating
ROE (6.99%) and ROCE (10.6%) both well below Minervini quality thresholds
Institutional holding declining every quarter for 4 straight quarters (27.4% → 20.65%) — sponsorship exiting, not building
RS percentile of 4 places this stock near the bottom of the entire universe on relative strength
verdict.biggest_risk: OTHER_INCOME_DRIVEN — reported earnings acceleration is materially inflated by non-operating income, and combined with negative cash conversion (POOR_CASH_CONVERSION), the earnings quality is not investment-grade even before considering the technical failure
